decision_note: Owner scope review plus live LLM Scout: index.html already works as a broad home-hub with clear routing into progression, research, heroes, events, PvP, and economy. Bing signal is useful, but query data is still broad/sparse and live Scout placed this topic in monitoring. Keep out of content intake until repeated Bing/GSC evidence shows a stable navigation-query pattern or homepage underperformance relative to other hubs.

topic_snapshot.title: Bing opportunity review: Last Z Guides — Research, Events, HQ, Heroes, and F2P Strategy
topic_snapshot.source_reference: Bing weekly 2026-05-01: page opportunity and query-page signals
topic_snapshot.notes: Useful signal, but the homepage is too broad and the query data is sparse; the opportunity is real yet should be monitored rather than acted on immediately as a rewrite target. Future trigger: If repeated Bing/GSC signals show a stable last z navigation/query pattern and the homepage underperforms relative to other hubs.
topic_snapshot.next_step: If repeated Bing/GSC signals show a stable last z navigation/query pattern and the homepage underperforms relative to other hubs.
evidence: Bing page signal: index.html had 1157 impressions, 32 clicks, 2.77% CTR, avg position 6.00.
Low CTR query: `last z` had 337 impressions, 1 clicks, 0.30% CTR, position 7.00.
Rising query: `last z` gained 75 impressions in the last 7-day comparison window.
Rising query: `last z guide` gained 17 impressions in the last 7-day comparison window.
Rising query: `last z survival shooter guide` gained 2 impressions in the last 7-day comparison window.

site_fit.primary_user_job: Improve query-to-page match and first-screen usefulness for `last z` searchers.
site_fit.archetype_reason: Existing page is `home-hub` in the `Home` cluster; Scout should prefer updating the existing page before proposing new content.

constraints: Use existing page template and navigation patterns.
Treat Bing data as a signal, not proof that a rewrite is needed.
Existing backlog history for this page: research-atlas-home-promotion:done.
reject_if: The query intent is already better served by another canonical page.
The proposed change would blur cluster role separation.
The improvement cannot be expressed without changing a cornerstone page beyond the approved scope.
